user_message: Can you help me run the hygiene check? The customer (Consolidated Messenger) is asking about partner engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 15:12 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 17:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger partner engagement

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is partner engagement.

[00:05:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:14:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:24:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:26:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-L -- Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
